response: The internet originated from 1960s U.S. Department of Defense-funded computer networking research, with its precursor ARPANET first demonstrated in 1969 via a message sent from UCLA to Stanford Research Institute; 1983’s transition to TCP/IP protocols established its modern technical foundation, and Tim Berners-Lee’s 1989 invention of the World Wide Web (at CERN, with HTML, URLs, HTTP) plus 1993’s Mosaic browser made it accessible to non-academic, non-research users. Pivotal post-accessibility milestones included the late-1990s dot-com boom (and 2001 crash) that spawned enduring giants Amazon and Google, 2006’s AWS launch catalyzing cloud computing, 2007’s iPhone popularizing mobile internet, and mid-2000s social media (Facebook, Twitter, YouTube) revolutionizing global communication. Today, the internet is a ubiquitous global infrastructure connecting over 5 billion people, underpinning nearly every facet of modern life from commerce to entertainment to interpersonal communication.
